macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Raw response
items.summary: No relevant macroeconomic or stock market news was found in the provided search results for the past 24 hours. The available results [1][2] only report on earthquakes in Venezuela and Japan, with no information regarding major index movements, Federal Reserve or central bank decisions, or significant company earnings and announcements. Consequently, no data on major index movements, Fed/central bank news, or significant company earnings can be provided from these sources.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $60K as Inflation Data Sparks Crypto Sell-Off
items.summary: Bitcoin fell back below $60,000 per token on Thursday after May's hot Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation report, which rose to a three-year high, triggering over $1.5 billion in crypto liquidations and pushing Bitcoin to a fresh 20-month low of $58,000.

items.title: Record $10.83 Million Bitcoin Held at Loss Signals Potential Cycle Bottom
items.summary: Glassnode data reveals 10.83 million BTC are now held at a loss, matching the exact setup seen at cycle bottoms in 2019, 2020, and 2022, while $450 million in levered long positions were liquidated in just 60 minutes amid the crash.

items.title: Ethereum Plunges 68% as Crypto Market Crashes Over 17%
items.summary: Ethereum, typically the less volatile altcoin, dropped 68% while Bitcoin fell 53%, with over 178,000 traders wiped out as the broader crypto market crashed more than 17% in 24 hours amid massive liquidations.

items.title: US Senators Demand Probe into Alleged $500M Trump-Emirati Crypto Deal
items.summary: Democratic Senator Richard Blumenthal is advocating for an inquiry into allegations of a covert $500 million investment from an Emirati royal into the Trump family's cryptocurrency venture, which occurred just four days before Trump's 2025 inauguration.

items.title: Binance Faces EU Service Halt Risk Without MiCA License by July 1
items.summary: Binance may be unable to service EU clients starting July 1 if it fails to secure a MiCA license, with analysts warning that without a special dispensation, the exchange could effectively lose its ability to operate in the European Union.

items.title: Law Firm Opens Investigation into MicroStrategy Over Securities Claims
items.summary: A law firm has launched an investigation into MicroStrategy regarding securities claims, adding to market contention as the stock dipped below $100 to $92 per share amid the broader crypto capitulation.

items.title: Coinbase Preparing New AI Trading Agent Rollout for July
items.summary: Coinbase is rolling out a new AI trading agent feature expected to launch in July, though it is not yet fully ready, amid growing US government contention over AI trading agents and potential regulatory involvement.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils Jalapeño AI Chip and Updates GPT-5.5; GPT-5.6 Delayed to July
items.summary: OpenAI announced its first custom AI inference chip, Jalapeño, designed from scratch for large language models, while releasing an instant update to GPT-5.5 and delaying the launch of GPT-5.6 from this week to July.

items.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Orchestrating Massive AI Distillation Campaign
items.summary: Anthropic made explosive accusations that Alibaba-linked operators conducted one of the largest AI distillation campaigns ever, creating thousands of accounts to harvest Claude's capabilities, alongside reports of Google DeepMind talent exodus to Anthropic.

items.title: xAI Fact-Checks Grok 5's 6-Trillion-Parameter Claim and Multi-Agent Features
items.summary: xAI confirmed Grok 5 is a Mixture-of-Experts model with approximately 6 trillion parameters, featuring dynamic agent spawning and persistent memory, though the model's release timeline and benchmark performance against GPT-4o and Claude are under scrutiny.

items.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro Delayed Due to Poor Checkpoint Tests; Google DeepMind Talent Exodus Continues
items.summary: Google delayed the release of Gemini 3.5 Pro following disappointing checkpoint tests, while reports indicate a growing number of Google DeepMind engineers are leaving to join Anthropic.

items.title: Optics 2026.5 Released with Native Photo Editing Integration and New ML Workflows
items.summary: Optics 2026.5 launched with native support for Apple Photos, Capture One, and Affinity, plus updated UpResML for offline upscaling and FaceML with new nose and neck segmentation features.

items.title: Micron Stock Surges 16% on Blowout Q3 AI Memory Results; Investors Worry About AI Capex Cycle
items.summary: Micron's fiscal Q3 revenue quadrupled year-over-year to $41 billion with 81% gross margins, driving a 16% stock surge, while investors express concern over the sustainability of endless AI capital expenditure spending.

items.title: Maine Town of Jay Overturns Data Center Ban to Host AI Facility
items.summary: Residents of Jay, Maine, successfully rallied to overturn a state-level data center ban, allowing real estate developer Tony MacDonald to convert a former plant into an AI data center.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Announces Mass Layoffs, Destiny Team Disbanded Amid Future Uncertainty
items.summary: Bungie has officially announced mass layoffs, with most of the Destiny team gone, though both Bungie and Sony express encouragement for future creativity and opportunities as the franchise enters early incubation for new projects.

items.title: Netflix Gaming Service Expands with 13,000+ Ratings, Featuring Stranger Things and RollerCoaster Tycoon
items.summary: Netflix's mobile gaming service, which includes titles like Stranger Things from 1984, RollerCoaster Tycoon, and Knives Out games, has garnered over 13,000 ratings, integrating mobile games into interactive stories tied to TV shows.

items.title: MSI Claw 8 EX AI+ Claims 11 Hours of Gameplay at 1080p Low with AI Upscaling
items.summary: The MSI Claw 8 EX AI+ handheld gaming device claims up to 11 hours of gameplay at 1080p low settings using AI mode and power presets, though the claim includes multiple asterisks regarding performance conditions.

items.title: Caitlin Clark's Game-Winning Performance Sparks Coach's Outburst on Official Disrespect
items.summary: Following Caitlin Clark's incredible game-winning performance, her coach publicly expressed frustration over the disrespect Clark has faced throughout the season, highlighting ongoing officiating controversies in women's basketball.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Announces Major 2027 Product Roadbook Including iPhone 20, Smart Glasses, and Tabletop Robot
items.summary: Apple's soon-to-be CEO John Ternus confirmed an ambitious 2027 roadmap featuring the iPhone 20, AirPods with cameras, smart glasses, and a tabletop robot, while Tim Cook acknowledged unavoidable price hikes due to global memory shortages driven by AI infrastructure demand.

items.title: Apple and Intel Agree to Co-Design Chips in the US, Boosting Intel Stock 9%
items.summary: President Trump announced that Apple has agreed to work with Intel to design and produce chips in the United States, causing Intel stock to surge over 9% in pre-market trading amid the global chip shortage.

items.title: Apple Car Project Officially Ends as Whimo Purchases Apple's Arizona Proving Ground
items.summary: Whimo has purchased Apple's 5,500-acre self-driving car test facility in Whitman, Arizona, which Apple originally bought in 2021 for $125 million, signaling the definitive end of Apple's autonomous vehicle project.

items.title: Samsung Develops Revolutionary 40,000-Nit Micro OLED Displays for Next-Gen VR/AR
items.summary: Samsung is creating micro OLED displays capable of 40,000 nits brightness—ten times brighter than current VR headsets—with mass production planned for 2028, potentially enabling ultra-light mixed reality glasses.
items.source: YouTube (Steam Machine Discussion & The Future of Augmented Reality)

items.title: Meta, Snap, and Qualcomm Race to Develop Sleek AR Glasses Despite Form Factor Challenges
items.summary: Industry giants Meta, Snap, and Qualcomm are accelerating efforts to create powerful, sleek AR glasses by tackling waveguide technology, eye tracking, and power efficiency to eliminate bulky compute pucks.
items.source: YouTube (Steam Machine Discussion & The Future of Augmented Reality)

items.title: Bungie Announces Mass Layoffs Hitting the Destiny Team as Monument of Triumph Update Launches
items.summary: Bungie has announced significant layoffs affecting most of the Destiny development team just as the new Monument of Triumph update for Destiny 2 was released, marking a major shift for the studio.
items.source: YouTube (This is the Last Destiny Weekly Update... Ever.)

items.title: Germany Scraps World's Largest Frigate Project After €2bn Wasted
items.summary: Germany has officially abandoned its world's largest frigate project after wasting approximately €2 billion, representing a major setback in European defense manufacturing.
